title: riedlea eriantha
intro: Riedlea eriantha, also known as the Mountain Pepper, is a flowering plant native to New South Wales, Australia, and the islands of the Pacific, belonging to the family Rutaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Riedlea eriantha include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and act as a natural antibacterial agent. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being used fresh or dried in various culinary and medicinal applications.
